nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Battle of Hastings
historicEvents.description: Battle of Hastings was fought between the Norman-French army and the battle-wary English army on October 14, 1066. Duke William of Normandy believed he was the rightful king rather than Harold, who had been crowned king several months earlier. Harold was killed in battle and William was crowned king on Christmas Day 1066.

historicEvents.title: Domesday Book
historicEvents.description: Domesday Book is Britain's earliest extant public record with information containing land surveys and landholdings commissioned by William I. The book was completed in 1086.

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: Elizabeth was born as the daughter of Father of Elizabeth and Mother of Elizabeth. She married Oliver Dinham III de Sir Lord of Dinham ** in 1144, in Hartland, Devon, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died in 1178, in Hartland, Devon, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Elizabeth est née, fille de Father of Elizabeth et Mother of Elizabeth. Elle a épousé Oliver Dinham III de Sir Lord of Dinham ** en 1144, à Hartland, Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée en 1178, à Hartland, Devon, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Elizabeth wurde, als Tochter von Father of Elizabeth und Mother of Elizabeth geboren. Sie hat 1144 in Hartland, Devon, England, United Kingdom Oliver Dinham III de Sir Lord of Dinham ** geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb 1178 in Hartland, Devon, England.

it.lifeSummary: Elizabeth è nata il come figlia di Father of Elizabeth e Mother of Elizabeth. Lei sposato Oliver Dinham III de Sir Lord of Dinham ** nel 1144, nel Hartland, Devon, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1178, nel Hartland, Devon, England.

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth(은)는 Father of Elizabeth(와)과 Mother of Elizabeth의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1144년 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 하틀랜드에서 Oliver Dinham III de Sir Lord of Dinham **(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1178년 영국 데폰 하틀랜드에서 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Elizabeth родилась. Её родителей звали Father of Elizabeth и Mother of Elizabeth. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Oliver Dinham III de Sir Lord of Dinham ** в 1144 г. в местности хартланд, девон, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла в 1178 г. в местности хартланд, девон, Англия.
